regime.label: Risk-On (Strong)
regime.description: Regime upgraded to full Risk-On after Apple's blowout Q2 and the S&P closing April as its best month since 2020 (+10%). S&P 500 and Nasdaq both closed at all-time highs Thursday. Apple beat on revenue ($111.18B, +17%), EPS ($2.01 vs $1.95 est), Services (all-time record $31B), and guide (Q3 14-17% vs 9.5% expected). Iran sent a revised proposal through Pakistani mediators Friday — oil fell ~5% (WTI ~$100, Brent ~$107) on de-escalation hopes. Trump says 'no one knows' the status of talks. Risk-on is now broad: Russell 2000 +2.21% Friday, every S&P sector positive Thursday. Key remaining risks: Hormuz still closed, Core PCE 3.2%, hawkish Fed dissenters vocal again Friday.

dominant_narratives.headline: Iran Ceasefire Holds in Name Only — Hormuz Remains a Battleground
dominant_narratives.body: The Apr 8 Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire nominally held through last week, but the strategic picture deteriorated daily. Both the US Navy and Iran seized commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, oil tanker traffic remains at near-standstill, and a second round of peace talks in Islamabad collapsed almost before it began.

updates.label: Mon–Wed Apr 21–23
updates.headline: Trump extends ceasefire; talks stall; Hormuz seizures intensify
updates.body: Tuesday evening Trump extended the ceasefire citing Iran's 'seriously fractured' government. Extension came with a 3–5 day ultimatum. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf said a full ceasefire only made sense if the Hormuz reopened. Wednesday saw Iran seize two foreign cargo ships inside the strait. Brent crude topped $105 Thursday on dual-blockade fears.
bullets: Ship traffic: Roughly 20M bbl/day transited Hormuz pre-war; current flow is a fraction of normal.
Naval escalation: US seized three ships under the blockade. Iran captured two cargo vessels.

market_impact.text: S&P dipped 0.4% Wednesday before recovering Thursday on earnings. Oil vol elevated. Energy sector beneficiary; transports and consumer discretionary exposed to inflation pass-through.

sources.label: CNBC — Iran oil price Strait Hormuz Apr 23
Wikipedia — 2026 Iran war ceasefire chronology

updates.label: Weekend — Update 2
updates.headline: Trump cancels Witkoff/Kushner Islamabad trip — peace hopes collapse Sunday AM
updates.body: Iran's FM Araghchi traveled to Pakistan Friday, then flew to Oman Saturday. On Sunday morning Trump announced he was canceling his envoys' planned trip to Islamabad, telling Fox News he would not send his team on 'an 18-hour flight to sit around talking about nothing.' Monday morning had to reprice the geopolitical component of the equity tape.
bullets: IEA assessment: IEA head Birol called this 'the biggest energy security threat in history.'

Israel-Lebanon: Trump Thursday announced a 3-week extension to the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire — a stabilizing signal — but Israel's PM simultaneously ordered vigorous attacks on Hezbollah. Lebanese Health Ministry death toll since March 2 now stands above 2,496.

sources.label: CNN Live — Trump cancels Witkoff/Kushner Pakistan trip Apr 25
Al Jazeera — Iran war live, Apr 26 AM

updates.label: Update 3 — Mon–Tue Apr 28
updates.headline: Iran submits formal Hormuz proposal — nuclear bypass offer; US skeptical; Brent $111; UAE exits OPEC
updates.body: Iran's FM Araghchi flew to Moscow Monday to meet Putin before the proposal reached Washington. Tehran's new offer: reopen Hormuz and end hostilities if the US lifts its naval blockade — but nuclear program negotiations deferred to a later stage. Rubio called it 'better' but indicated nuclear deferral is a non-starter. CNN reports Trump unlikely to accept.
bullets: UAE exits OPEC effective May 1: First major Gulf exit since Qatar in 2019. UAE produced 3.4M bbl/day pre-war but was cut to 1.9M bbl/day by Hormuz closure.
Oil 7th straight up session: Brent topped $111 Tuesday — highest since March. WTI surpassed $98. National average gas hit $4.18/gallon.
UN Security Council: Guterres warned standoff risks triggering a global food emergency. Qatar warned against a 'frozen conflict.'

market_impact.text: Monday: S&P 7,173.91 (+0.12%, ATH close), Nasdaq 24,887.10 (+0.20%, ATH close). Tuesday reversed — Nasdaq −1%, S&P −0.49% on OpenAI miss + oil rise.

sources.label: NBC News — US cool on Iran proposal, Brent hits 3-week high Apr 28
The National — UAE announces OPEC exit Apr 28
Bloomberg — UAE to leave OPEC and OPEC+ in May Apr 28

updates.label: Update 4 — Wed Apr 29 AM
updates.headline: Trump 'No More Mr. Nice Guy' — Rubio rejects proposal AND questions Araghchi's authority — Brent $115
updates.body: At 4am ET Wednesday, Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Iran can't get their act together. They don't know how to sign a non-nuclear deal. They better get smart soon!' accompanied by an AI gun image captioned 'NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!' WSJ reports Trump has told aides to prepare for an extended blockade.
bullets: Rubio authority question: Rubio told Fox News he questions whether Araghchi 'had the authority to submit that offer' — meaning the US now doubts whether any FM deal can be honored by the Supreme Leader.
Bessent: Iran oil pumping 'will soon collapse': Treasury Secretary posted on X the US blockade is 'creaking' Iran's oil industry. Signals the strategy is economic strangulation.
Oil: Brent $115+ (highest since June 2022), WTI $103+ — 8th straight session. Both up 49%+ since war began Feb 28.
IRGC counter-threat: Deputy for Political Affairs warned of 'surprise tactics' that would 'set massive vessels ablaze' in case of any US miscalculation.

market_impact.text: Hyperscalers all 1–2% lower premarket. Brent $115 on FOMC day. S&P futures +0.22%, Nasdaq futures +0.50% — market not in full retreat but Iran-exposed AI names selling into earnings.

sources.label: The National — Trump 'No More Mr. Nice Guy' Iran warning Apr 29
ABC7 — Rubio dismisses Iran proposal, questions authority; Bessent warning Apr 29
CNBC — Oil extends rally, Brent tops $114, WTI above $103 Apr 29

updates.label: Update 5 — Wed–Thu Apr 29–30
updates.headline: Trump formally rejects Iran proposal — 'no nuclear weapons, ever' — Ghalibaf says oil heading to $140 — revised proposal expected Friday
updates.body: In a phone interview with Axios Wednesday afternoon, Trump formally and publicly rejected Iran's Hormuz-without-nuclear proposal: 'at this moment, there will never be a deal unless they agree that there will be no nuclear weapons.' WSJ confirmed he told aides to prepare for extended blockade. Brent peaked at $116.53 Wed (+4.74%) — highest since June 2022. WTI above $104. Pakistani sources tell CNN that Iran's revised proposal may arrive by Friday.
bullets: Ghalibaf: oil to $140 — mocked Bessent's 'pumping will collapse' prediction, citing 'three days in, no well exploded.' Said 'Next stop: 140' on Brent.
IRGC escalation: Daily kinetic threat language. Deputy for Political Affairs warned of 'surprise tactics' and ability to 'set massive vessels ablaze.'
Revised proposal expected Friday: CNN sources say Pakistan may receive revised Iranian framework by end of week.

market_impact.text: Oil pulled back slightly from $116 peak as hyperscaler earnings provided risk-on offset. WTI ~$103-104, Brent ~$114-116. Formal rejection removes near-term diplomatic ceiling on oil upside. Friday revised proposal is the next Iran catalyst.

sources.label: CNN — Trump rejects Iran proposal, Iran-Ukraine timetable, Day 61 Apr 29
ABC7 — Ghalibaf 'Next stop 140'; Bessent oil collapse warning Apr 29
Trading Economics — Brent crude $116.53 high Apr 29 (+4.74%)

updates.label: Live Update
updates.headline: Iran sends revised proposal via Pakistan — oil drops 5% — Trump says 'no one knows' — CENTCOM prepared strike plan — War Powers deadline today
updates.body: Iran sent an updated peace proposal to Pakistani mediators Friday morning. Pakistani officials confirmed mediators received the revised framework and it has been delivered to the US. Oil fell sharply on the news — WTI crude fell nearly 5% to $100.03, Brent lost nearly 3% to $107.14 by mid-morning. Trump, speaking Friday, said 'no one knows' the status of talks with Iran as the S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time high. The administration simultaneously argued the ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities for War Powers Resolution purposes, avoiding the May 1 congressional deadline.
bullets: Revised proposal delivered: Pakistani officials confirmed to MS Now that mediators received an updated framework from Iran. The proposal has been delivered to the US. No details of its contents have been disclosed, but it follows Trump's formal rejection of the prior offer Wednesday.

Oil -5% on hopes: WTI fell to $100.03 (from $103+ Thursday), Brent to $107.14 (from $114-116 range). Context: Brent hit a four-year high of $126.41 on Thursday before settling at $114.01. Friday's drop is significant but oil remains far above pre-war levels.

CENTCOM strike plan: Axios reported Thursday that Trump received a briefing from CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper on a 'short and powerful' wave of strikes against Iran — including infrastructure — as a pressure option if talks fail. The plan exists; it has not been ordered.

War Powers deadline: The 60-day War Powers Resolution clock (started March 2) hit May 1. The White House argues the ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities, making the deadline moot. Democratic senators Kaine and Warren disagree. Hegseth argued the same before the House Armed Services Committee.

Gas $4.39/gallon: National average hit a new war-era high Thursday per AAA. This is the inflation pressure the FOMC hawkish dissenters (Hammack/Kashkari, who pushed back again Friday) are flagging as incompatible with any near-term rate cut.

market_impact.text: Oil -5% on revised proposal news is the dominant early catalyst. S&P +0.54%, Nasdaq +0.69%, Russell +2.21%. Apple +3.5% pulling everything higher. The combination of Apple's blowout + Iran diplomatic re-engagement + oil pullback is the trifecta driving the risk-on regime upgrade. Watch: if Trump responds to the revised proposal positively over the weekend, oil could fall another $5-10 Monday and cement the Risk-On (Strong) regime.

sources.label: CNBC — Oil prices fall after Iran sends updated peace proposal to mediators in Pakistan May 1
CBS News — Iran war, Trump mulls latest Iranian offer, War Powers deadline May 1
TheStreet — Stock market today May 1: S&P Nasdaq records, Apple, oil falls

dominant_narratives.headline: Intel's Best Day Since 1987 Reignites the AI CPU Thesis — and Drags the Whole Chip Sector
dominant_narratives.body: Intel reported Q1 revenue of $13.6B against consensus of $12.4B — a $1.2B beat — with adj. EPS of $0.29 vs. $0.01 expected. Data center and AI revenue accelerated sequentially, the Xeon 6+ ramp confirmed CPU is now an infrastructure play in agentic AI deployments, and Q2 guidance of $13.8–$14.8B blew past $13.1B consensus. Shares closed up 24% Friday, the best single-session gain since October 1987. The broader read-through was immediate: AMD +13%, Qualcomm +10%, SOXX ETF posted its 18th consecutive positive session and ended the week +11%. Nvidia crossed the $5 trillion market cap threshold again on Friday.

dominant_narratives.headline: Retail Taking Profits on Intel; Institutional Conviction Drives the Index
dominant_narratives.body: VandaTrack reported retail sold $18M+ of Intel into the 24% surge — disciplined exit, not FOMO. Fear & Greed recovered from 15 (Extreme Fear) to 72 (Greed) in 30 days. The week of Apr 21–25: Nasdaq +1.5%, S&P +0.6%, Dow -0.4% — technology-led advance, narrowing breadth.

updates.label: Update 1 — Tue Apr 28
updates.headline: OpenAI revenue miss hits AI capex thesis — Monday ATH reversed sharply Tuesday
updates.body: WSJ reported Monday evening that OpenAI missed its own internal revenue and user-growth targets, and CFO Sarah Friar warned leadership the company may struggle to pay future computing contracts if top-line doesn't expand. Oracle (holding a $300B, 5-year OpenAI compute contract) dropped 7.5% premarket Tuesday. Nvidia, Broadcom, AMD fell 2–5%.
bullets: Tuesday confirmed closes: S&P 7,138.80 (−0.49%), Nasdaq 24,663.80 (−0.9%), Dow 49,141.93 (−0.05%), Russell 2000 −1.15%. 10Y yield rose to 4.36% — a 4-week high.
Oracle defended OpenAI: Oracle spokesperson said it's 'incredibly excited' about the OpenAI partnership and sees 'continued momentum.'

sources.label: CNBC — S&P 500/Nasdaq Tue Apr 28 confirmed closes
TheStreet — Stock market today Apr 28 confirmed closes

updates.label: Update 2 — Wed Apr 29 AM
updates.headline: Hyperscalers 1–2% lower premarket — AI capex verdict arrives tonight — S&P futures slightly higher
updates.body: All four major hyperscalers trading 1–2% lower premarket Wednesday as the OpenAI revenue miss overhang combines with Iran escalation heading into their earnings prints. S&P futures +0.22%, Nasdaq futures +0.50% — market not in full retreat but hyperscalers specifically being sold ahead of earnings.
bullets: Microsoft tonight: Azure guided 37–38% growth. Beat threshold 39%+. Must address OpenAI demand weakness directly.
Alphabet/Meta/Amazon tonight: AI revenue attribution — how much cloud growth is directly AI-driven — is the decisive question.
Defensive rotation: Coca-Cola +4% Tuesday on strong earnings. Consumer staples gaining vs tech.

sources.label: Trading Economics — US stock market Apr 29 futures, hyperscaler premarket
Yahoo Finance — Microsoft Q3 FY26 earnings preview Apr 29

updates.label: Update 3 — Wed Close / Thu AM
updates.headline: Hyperscaler earnings: beat-and-raise broadly — Alphabet +7% AH, Meta −5% on flat Q2 guide — MSFT +3% AH — Dow 5th straight loss
updates.body: All four hyperscalers reported after Wednesday's close. Results broadly positive on AI revenue but created bifurcated after-hours reactions. Wednesday closed little-changed (S&P −0.04%, Nasdaq +0.04%) but the Dow fell for a fifth straight session (−0.57% to 48,861.81) on oil's drag. The 10Y yield surged 5+ bps to 4.41% after the Fed decision.
bullets: Microsoft (MSFT) — Strong beat: Revenue $82.9B (+18%), Azure +40% (crushed 37-38% guidance, re-accelerated from 39%), AI annual run rate $37B (+123% YoY). Capex $31.9B — below the $34.9B estimate. Stock +3-4% AH.

Alphabet (GOOGL) — Blockbuster: Revenue $109.9B (+22%, best growth since 2022), Cloud $20B (+63%), net income +81% to $62.6B. CFO said 2027 capex will 'significantly increase.' Cloud backlog $460B. Stock +6-7% AH.
Meta — Mixed reaction: Revenue $56.3B (+33%), net income +61% to $26.8B. BUT Q2 revenue guide flat vs expectations — stock fell 5%+ AH. Capex raised to $125–$145B (up from $115–$135B).
Amazon (AMZN) — Softer: AWS +28% to $37.6B, total revenue +17% to $181.5B. Q2 guidance softer — stock -3% AH. $16.8B pre-tax gain from Anthropic investment boosted net income.

market_impact.text: AI capex ROI narrative partially rehabilitated (Azure +40%, Google Cloud +63%) but not fully resolved (Meta flat Q2 guide, Amazon softer). Futures pointing modestly higher Thursday morning. GDP + Core PCE have now printed: GDP 2.0%, Core PCE 3.2%.

sources.label: InvestmentNews — MSFT/META/GOOGL/AMZN Q1 2026 earnings recap
NBC News — Big Tech earnings beat but fell short of sky-high AI expectations Apr 29
SEC 8-K — Amazon Q1 2026 (AWS +28%, Anthropic $16.8B gain)

updates.label: Live Update
updates.headline: S&P and Nasdaq hit all-time highs Thursday — April best month since 2020 — Apple +3.5% Fri — Russell leads
updates.body: Thursday Apr 30 closed at record highs: S&P 7,207.xx (+1.02%, first-ever close above 7,200), Nasdaq record close (+0.89%), Dow +1.62% (+850pts), Russell 2000 +2.1% (best performer). S&P finished April +10% — best single month since November 2020. Every sector of the S&P 500 advanced Thursday. Caterpillar +10% (beat) and Alphabet +9% led; Meta -7.5% and Microsoft -3.8% were the main drags but couldn't stop the tape.
bullets: Friday May 1 early: S&P +0.54%, Dow +0.37%, Nasdaq +0.69%, Russell 2000 +2.21%. Apple +3.5% premarket after blowout earnings carrying the Nasdaq.

Energy major earnings: XOM and CVX both beat Wall Street expectations but profits fell sharply — XOM net income -45%, CVX -36% — due to oil price surge since Feb 28 causing accounting charges and hedging losses. Gas $4.39/gallon national average, up 42-44% since war began.

SanDisk (SNDK) crushed estimates: Revenue $5.95B, EPS $23.41 vs $14.50 expected, gross margin ~78%. CEO: 'fundamental inflection point' tied to datacenter mix shift. Shares +73% over past month — the hidden AI infrastructure winner of earnings season.

Market breadth improving: S&P equal-weight rose <6% in April vs cap-weight +10% — rally still mega-cap led. Russell 2000 outperforming Friday is the first sign of broadening. Watch whether Apple's follow-through pulls laggards into May.

market_impact.text: The combination of Apple blowout + Iran revised proposal + oil -5% is driving the broadest risk-on session since the Intel earnings week. Russell +2.21% suggests rotation is beginning. The risk: rally still mega-cap concentrated. Two Fed dissenters (Hammack, Kashkari) pushed back on any dovish FOMC read Friday — the rate path remains live.

sources.label: TheStreet — Stock market today May 1: Nasdaq S&P 500 jump again after record closes
CNBC — S&P 500 rises to record to kick off May trading as oil falls, Apple shares jump
24/7 Wall St — S&P 500 strong May footing powered by Apple, SanDisk, easing oil
TheStreet — Stock market today Apr 30: S&P caps best month since 2020 as Alphabet rallies

dominant_narratives.headline: FOMC Tuesday–Wednesday: Hold Certain, but the Statement Tone Is the Variable
dominant_narratives.body: Fed meets Apr 28–29. Hold at 3.50–3.75% unanimous. Powell's press conference is the variable — how he characterizes stagflation risk from the Hormuz blockade, and whether he shifts language toward 'higher for longer,' will hit rate-sensitive stocks. March FOMC minutes: 'further progress in reducing inflation had been absent in recent months.'

updates.label: Update 1 — Tue Apr 28
updates.headline: Powell's final FOMC as chair — Warsh Senate vote 10am same day — market prices no cuts until Sept 2027
updates.body: Three developments materially changed what Wednesday means. First: this is Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting as chair — term expires May 15. Second: Senate Banking Committee votes on Warsh at 10am ET Wednesday, 4 hours before the decision. Third: CME FedWatch now shows no cuts until September 2027.
bullets: Warsh path cleared: DOJ dropped Powell investigation Friday. Tillis lifted block. Republicans hold 13–11 majority. EY-Parthenon expects Warsh confirmed before May 15, chairing the June FOMC.
Warsh regime-change signals: Fewer annual meetings, new inflation framework. RSM Brusuelas: 'Investors are not pricing in a rate cut this year.'

sources.label: CBS News — Fed rate decision April 2026, Powell final meeting
Reuters/WHBL — Senate Banking Committee Warsh vote April 29
Blockonomi — Warsh confirmation, CME no-cut-til-2027

updates.label: Update 2 — Wed Apr 29
updates.headline: Decision arrives 2pm today — Warsh vote this morning — Powell final press conference 2:30pm
updates.body: Today is the day. The Warsh Senate Banking Committee vote is this morning at 10am ET. The FOMC rate decision arrives at 2pm — hold at 3.50–3.75% is 100% priced. Powell's last press conference as chair begins at 2:30pm.
bullets: Brent $115 at press conference time: Powell faces questions about a supply shock the FOMC cannot address with rate cuts. His exact language on the energy shock changes the balance of risks.
Will Powell confirm staying as Fed Governor through 2028? The DOJ investigation was dropped Friday, removing his stated precondition.
Powell press conference as valedictory: The usual forward guidance read-through is diminished — this is more about tone and legacy.

market_impact.text: With the rate decision zero-surprise, the market-moving variables are: (1) dovish language from Powell contradicting 'no cuts until 2027' — bullish; (2) stagflation acknowledgment on Iran — bearish; (3) Powell's answer on remaining as Governor.

sources.label: CNBC — Fed meeting preview April 2026, Powell legacy Apr 28
Kiplinger — April Fed meeting live updates Apr 29

updates.label: Update 3 — Wed Close — RESULTS
updates.headline: FOMC held 3.5–3.75% in dramatic 8-4 split — Powell stays as Fed Governor — 'Trump's legal attacks left me no choice'
updates.body: The Federal Reserve held at 3.50–3.75% in an 8-4 split — the widest dissent margin in years. Stephen Miran wanted to cut rates; Beth Hammack, Neel Kashkari, and Lorie Logan opposed the inclusion of an easing bias in the statement — meaning they want the language to reflect that cuts are further away, not closer.
bullets: Powell stays as Fed Governor: His exact words: 'Trump's legal attacks have left me no choice.' Powell will remain on the FOMC as a voting governor alongside Warsh starting June.
Warsh advanced 13-11 party-line: All 13 Republicans in favor, all 11 Democrats against. Full Senate confirmation vote expected within weeks.
Hawkish dissent signal: KKM Financial CEO Kilburg on CNBC: the three dissenters are 'sending a warning to Trump nominee Warsh — this is a new quarterback hitting the portal.'
March CPI context: Headline CPI rose 0.9% MoM and 3.3% YoY in March — highest annual increase since May 2024. Core PCE just printed 3.2% this morning.

market_impact.text: The 10Y yield surged 5+ bps to 4.41% immediately after the decision. Core PCE 3.2% this morning validates the hawkish dissenters retroactively. Powell staying as Governor means Warsh inherits internal tension from day one of the June FOMC.

sources.label: CNBC — Fed meeting live: Powell stays, 8-4 vote, Warsh advances Apr 29
Federal Reserve — FOMC Statement Apr 29, 2026 (8-4 vote breakdown)
Kiplinger — April Fed meeting recap: Powell stays, hawkish split vote Apr 29

updates.label: Live Update
updates.headline: Hammack and Kashkari push back Friday — gas $4.39/gallon — War Powers Resolution deadline today — rate path stays live
updates.body: Two of the three hawkish FOMC dissenters from Wednesday — Beth Hammack and Neel Kashkari — publicly pushed back on any dovish interpretation of the FOMC outcome Friday, citing oil-driven inflation risk from the Hormuz closure. Gas hit $4.39/gallon nationally Thursday — up 42-44% since the war began February 28. The War Powers Resolution May 1 deadline arrived today: White House argues the ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities for WPR purposes, meaning Trump does not need Congressional authorization. Democratic senators Kaine and Warren formally dispute this reading.
bullets: Hammack and Kashkari public pushback: Both told media Friday that the oil-driven inflation environment is incompatible with any near-term rate cut. This is the same position they voted on Wednesday but now stated publicly — a signal they will continue to advocate for hawkish policy under Warsh.

War Powers deadline: The May 1 WPR 60-day clock was triggered March 2. White House argues ceasefire terminated it. Hegseth argued the same before Congress Thursday. Democratic senators formally disagree — sets up a potential constitutional confrontation over the conflict's legal authorization.
Rate market: No meaningful repricing of the 'no cuts until Sept 2027+' path despite Apple blowout and S&P records. The 10Y yield remains elevated at 4.41%.

market_impact.text: Hawkish pushback from Hammack/Kashkari caps the bond rally that would normally accompany a risk-on equity day. 10Y holding near 4.41% limits how much rate-sensitive names (banks, utilities, homebuilders) can participate in the rally. The rate-path-vs-earnings tension is the defining macro setup for May.

sources.label: 24/7 Wall St — Hammack/Kashkari push back on dovish FOMC read, May 1
CNBC — War Powers Resolution deadline, White House ceasefire argument, May 1
CBS News — Hegseth, War Powers, gas $4.39, CENTCOM strike plan

dominant_narratives.headline: Consumer Sentiment at Record Low Even as Stocks Hit Records — Dangerous Divergence
dominant_narratives.body: The final University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment survey for April, released Friday, showed sentiment holding at record-low levels even as markets hit all-time highs and the ceasefire removed near-term war fears. This divergence between equity markets and consumer psychology is notable. Energy prices near $4/gallon nationally and elevated food costs from supply chain disruptions are hitting households in ways that don't show up in headline index performance. Energy Secretary Wright acknowledged on CNN Sunday that gas prices below $3/gallon 'might not happen until next year.' The Q4 2025 GDP revision to 0.5% from 1.4% remains a warning about the reliability of advance estimates.

catalyst_calendar.body: Trump canceled the Witkoff/Kushner Islamabad trip Sunday AM. Monday's open reflected weekend repricing of the geopolitical premium. Watch for any Truth Social posts from Trump on Iran, any Iranian response to the cancellation, and whether Araghchi's Oman visit yields a back-channel signal.

March reading came in at 91.8, fractionally above 91.0 prior but with a weak Expectations sub-index. April's reading will reflect the Iran conflict, oil shock, and ceasefire period. A further decline below 90 would strengthen the recession-risk narrative heading into Thursday's GDP print.

Result: Revenue $82.9B (+18%), Azure +40% (crushed 37-38% guidance, re-accelerated from 39%), AI annual run rate $37B (+123% YoY). Capex $31.9B — below $34.9B estimate. Nadella: 'Our AI business surpassed $37B run rate.' Directly neutralized the OpenAI miss narrative. Stock +3-4% AH.

catalyst_calendar.event: Senate Banking Committee: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair Confirmation Vote
catalyst_calendar.body: Result: Voted 13-11 along strict party lines — all 13 Republicans yes, all 11 Democrats no. Warsh advances to full Senate confirmation vote, expected within weeks. Powell congratulated Warsh in his final press conference remarks. EY-Parthenon expects Warsh confirmed before Powell's May 15 term end, chairing the June FOMC.

catalyst_calendar.event: FOMC Rate Decision + Powell Press Conference
catalyst_calendar.body: Result: Fed held 3.50–3.75% in a dramatic 8-4 split — widest dissent in years. Miran dissented wanting a cut; Hammack, Kashkari, and Logan dissented wanting the easing bias removed. Powell announced staying as Fed Governor: 'Trump's legal attacks have left me no choice.' 10Y surged 5+ bps to 4.41%. Core PCE 3.2% printed next morning validated the hawkish dissenters retroactively.

catalyst_calendar.event: Alphabet, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft Q1 Earnings — AI Capex Triple-Header
catalyst_calendar.body: Results: Microsoft: Azure +40% (beat 37-38% guide, re-accelerated), AI run rate $37B (+123%), capex below estimates — stock +3-4% AH. Alphabet: Cloud +63%, revenue +22% (best since 2022), net income +81% — stock +6-7% AH. Meta: revenue +33%, EPS +62%, BUT Q2 guide flat + capex raised to $125-145B — stock −5% AH. Amazon: AWS +28%, softer Q2 guidance, $16.8B Anthropic gain inflated headline — stock −3% AH.

catalyst_calendar.event: Q1 2026 GDP Advance Estimate + Core PCE March
catalyst_calendar.body: Results (8:30 ET today): Q1 GDP advance estimate: 2.0% annualized — beats 0.5% Q4, misses 2.3% consensus. Core PCE March: 3.2% YoY — sharply accelerating from 2.7% February. PCE headline 3.5% (from 2.8%). The combination is the stagflation configuration: growth below consensus, inflation re-accelerating. FOMC hawkish dissenters from yesterday are retroactively validated by the PCE acceleration.

Result: Revenue $111.18B (+17%, March quarter record, first time Q2 crossed $100B). EPS $2.01 vs $1.95 est. iPhone $57B (+22%, March record). Services $30.98B all-time high. Mac $8.4B. Gross margin 49.3% vs 48.4% est. Q3 guidance: 14-17% growth vs 9.5% est. $100B buyback authorized. Dividend raised to $0.27 (+4%). Cook: 'best March quarter ever.' Stock +3.5% Fri. Cook did not bring Ternus on the call. Supply constraints flagged for Q3 (memory crunch per Cook).

catalyst_calendar.event: University of Michigan Final Consumer Sentiment + ISM Manufacturing
catalyst_calendar.body: ISM Manufacturing PMI — a below-50 reading confirms goods sector contraction under the oil shock. Paired with Thursday's GDP 2.0% print. UMich Final Consumer Sentiment — record-low readings have been consistent; any bounce would be notable given S&P at all-time highs and Apple blowout.

Iran submitted a formal Hormuz-bypass proposal Monday (reopen strait, defer nuclear talks). US skeptical — Rubio says nuclear issue is non-negotiable upfront. Brent $111, WTI $98+, gas $4.18 national average. No face-to-face meetings currently scheduled. UAE exits OPEC effective May 1. UN Security Council called Monday for urgent Hormuz reopening. The ceasefire's status, any ship seizures, and any response from Trump on the Iranian proposal are continuous catalysts. Oil can move 3–7% on a single development.

indexes.note: Holding elevated — Hammack/Kashkari pushing back Fri

macro_fed.label: Powell
macro_fed.note: Votes alongside Warsh starting June FOMC

energy_volatility.label: WTI Crude
energy_volatility.note: Fell ~5% Fri on Iran revised proposal — was $103+ Thu

energy_volatility.label: National Gas

scenarios.label: A
scenarios.headline: Bull Case: Iran Blinks, Hormuz Cracks Open — Mag-7 Earnings Confirm AI ROI
scenarios.body: Araghchi's Oman visit produces a back-channel signal that Iran is prepared to reopen Hormuz under a modified framework. Trump reverses the talk-cancellation or frames it as a negotiating tactic. Oil drops $10–$15 on the news, WTI falls back to $82–$86 range. Simultaneously, Wed night's Alphabet/Meta/Amazon triple-header produces clean beat-and-raise results with AI revenue clearly accelerating vs. capex. Thursday's GDP comes in at 1.5%+ with core PCE holding at 2.7% — not good, but not worse. Fed reads as able to cut once in late 2026. Nasdaq pushes through 25,500. S&P targets 7,400–7,500 by Friday.

updates.label: Update — Fri May 1 — Bull Case Largely Triggered
updates.text: Iran sent revised proposal (oil -5%) ✓, Apple blowout beat-and-raise ✓, S&P and Nasdaq at all-time highs ✓, Russell broadening ✓, April best month since 2020 ✓. The bull case did not require full Hormuz reopening — just diplomatic re-engagement plus earnings confirmation. Both arrived. Hormuz remains closed (key miss vs. full bull case), and Core PCE 3.2% prevents the Fed flexibility scenario. Bull case is ~70% realized.

scenarios.label: B
scenarios.headline: Base Case: Ceasefire Holds Fragilly — Earnings Mixed — Fed Stays on Hold
scenarios.body: Trump's talk cancellation is absorbed as posturing, not breakdown. Iran makes no new military moves in Hormuz. Oil stays range-bound $92–$100. Wednesday earnings are mixed: 2 of 3 hyperscalers beat, 1 (likely Alphabet or Amazon) disappoints on cloud margins. FOMC holds at 3.50–3.75% and Powell's language is deliberately ambiguous. Q1 GDP comes in at 0.8–1.2%, soft but not contractionary. Core PCE at 2.7–2.8%, confirming stickiness but not acceleration. Market consolidates: S&P flat to +1%, Nasdaq -0.5 to +1%.

scenarios.label: C
scenarios.headline: Bear Case: Talks Collapse Fully — GDP Miss + PCE Hot = Stagflation Signal
scenarios.body: Trump's cancellation is not reversed. Iran retaliates by announcing expanded control of Hormuz transit. A naval incident pushes Brent to $110–$120. GDP advance estimate comes in below 0.5%, and March core PCE accelerates to 2.9–3.0% on oil pass-through. This is the stagflation configuration: growth slowing, inflation re-accelerating, Fed hands tied. Mag-7 earnings miss top-line estimates. S&P breaks below 6,900, VIX spikes to 28–32.

updates.label: Update — Tue Apr 28 — Bear Triggers Stacking
updates.text: Multiple bear-case conditions already in play: Brent $111 (bear range $110–$120 ✓), Trump cool on Iran proposal (talks stalled ✓), OpenAI miss (AI capex ROI crack ✓), UAE exits OPEC ✓, no Fed cuts until Sept 2027 ✓.

updates.label: Update — Wed Apr 29 AM — Diplomatic Path Now Closed
updates.text: Trump 'No More Mr. Nice Guy' and Rubio's authority-question framing closed the near-term diplomatic path. Brent entered the bear scenario range ($115 vs $110–$120). GDP + PCE were the final binaries.

updates.label: Update — Thu Apr 30 AM — Partial Bear / Partial Neutralization
updates.text: GDP 2.0% avoids the sub-0.5% bear case floor — the economy is not in freefall. But Core PCE 3.2% (from 2.7%) is the highest level in years driven by energy pass-through, confirming the stagflation pressure the bear case described. The hyperscaler beats (Alphabet, MSFT) partially neutralized the AI capex thesis risk. Net: bear case partially in play but not fully triggered — the regime sits between base and bear. The revised Iranian proposal expected Friday is the remaining potential catalyst in either direction.

key_questions.question: Does Trump reverse his Islamabad cancellation or does Iran present a new, modified framework through Oman's mediation channel that gets talks back on track this week?
key_questions.answer: Iran submitted a formal proposal (reopen Hormuz, defer nuclear talks). Rubio rejected it on nuclear grounds AND questioned whether Araghchi had authority to make the offer. Trump confirmed formal rejection to Axios Wednesday: 'there will never be a deal unless they agree there will be no nuclear weapons.' Bessent's 'pumping will soon collapse' confirms the US strategy is economic strangulation. Revised Iranian proposal expected Friday via Pakistan.

key_questions.question: Does Q1 2026 GDP advance estimate come in above 1.0% — confirming the economy is withstanding the oil shock — or below 0.5%, echoing the Q4 2025 revision pattern?
key_questions.answer: GDP advance estimate: 2.0% annualized — beats 0.5% Q4, misses 2.3% consensus. Economy not in freefall. Government spending (shutdown recovery, war expenditures) and data center investment carrying growth. Consumer demand weak. GDP is likely to be revised — the Q4 revision precedent from 1.4% to 0.5% applies.

key_questions.question: Does March core PCE accelerate above 2.8%, confirming oil pass-through has reached consumer prices and pushing the "higher for longer" Fed narrative forward?
key_questions.answer: Core PCE rose to 3.2% YoY (from 2.7% Feb) — well above 2.8% threshold. PCE headline 3.5% (from 2.8%). Energy pass-through confirmed. Strongest quarterly acceleration in core PCE since the 2022 inflation peak. The FOMC hawkish dissenters (Hammack/Kashkari/Logan) who wanted the easing bias removed are now validated by this print.

key_questions.question: Do Alphabet, Meta, and Amazon show that AI revenue is accelerating fast enough to validate hyperscaler capex — or does any one of the three disappoint on cloud margins while maintaining high spend?
key_questions.answer: Alphabet: clean beat (Cloud +63%, revenue +22% best since 2022). Microsoft: Azure re-accelerated to 40%, AI run rate $37B (+123%). Both strongly validated the thesis. Meta: beat EPS but flat Q2 revenue guide while raising capex to $125–145B — the exact capex-without-acceleration scenario this question identified. Amazon: AWS +28% but softer Q2 guidance; Anthropic $16.8B gain inflated the headline. Net: AI capex ROI partially validated, not fully resolved. The question remains open for Q2 reporting.

key_questions.question: Does Powell's press conference language Wednesday signal the FOMC is keeping rate-cut optionality for later 2026, or does the statement shift toward a "higher for longer" stance that prices out the remaining cut in the dot plot?
key_questions.answer: Hold delivered 8-4 split. Three hawkish dissenters (Hammack/Kashkari/Logan) wanted the easing bias removed — not to cut, but to signal rates stay higher for longer. Miran wanted a cut. Powell announced staying as Fed Governor: 'Trump's legal attacks have left me no choice.' Warsh advanced 13-11. 10Y surged 5+ bps to 4.41%. CME FedWatch: no cuts until September 2027+ confirmed. Core PCE 3.2% this morning validates the hawkish dissent position retroactively.

key_questions.question: Does the Hormuz dual-blockade situation produce a kinetic incident — ship-vs-ship — that forces market reassessment of ceasefire durability and drives a >5% oil spike in a single session?
key_questions.answer: No kinetic incident materialized through end of April. Iran sent a revised proposal via Pakistan Friday — oil fell ~5% on de-escalation hopes. The threat of a kinetic incident has not gone away (CENTCOM prepared a strike plan; IRGC continues kinetic threat language) but the diplomatic re-engagement on Friday reduces the immediate probability of an incident this weekend.

key_questions.question: Does Apple provide any guidance on the CEO transition timeline or product roadmap that signals whether the post-Cook era is a catalyst or a risk?
key_questions.answer: Apple Q2 FY26: Revenue $111.18B (+17%, March record), EPS $2.01 vs $1.95 est, iPhone $57B (+22%), Services $30.98B all-time high, gross margin 49.3% vs 48.4% est. Q3 guide 14-17% vs 9.5% expected — the biggest positive surprise of the call. $100B buyback, dividend to $0.27 (+4%). Cook did not bring Ternus on the call — CEO transition commentary minimal. Supply constraints (memory crunch) flagged for Q3 as the primary risk. Stock +3.5% Friday.
